records.ety: [OE. bodi, AS. bodig; akin to OHG. botah. √257. Cf. Bodice.]

defs: The material organized substance of an animal, whether living or dead, as distinguished from the spirit, or vital principle; the physical person.
quotes: Absent in body, but present in spirit.
For of the soul the body form doth take. For soul is form, and doth the body make.

defs: The trunk, or main part, of a person or animal, as distinguished from the limbs and head; the main, central, or principal part, as of a tree, army, country, etc.
quotes: Who set the body and the limbs Of this great sport together?
The van of the king's army was led by the general; . . . in the body was the king and the prince.
Rivers that run up into the body of Italy.

defs: The real, as opposed to the symbolical; the substance, as opposed to the shadow.
quotes: Which are a shadow of things to come; but the body is of Christ.

defs: A person; a human being; -- frequently in composition; as, anybody, nobody.
quotes: A dry, shrewd kind of a body.

defs: A number of individuals spoken of collectively, usually as united by some common tie, or as organized for some purpose; a collective whole or totality; a corporation; as, a legislative body; a clerical body.
quotes: A numerous body led unresistingly to the slaughter.

defs: A number of things or particulars embodied in a system; a general collection; as, a great body of facts; a body of laws or of divinity.
Any mass or portion of matter; any substance distinct from others; as, a metallic body; a moving body; an aëriform body.
quotes: By collision of two bodies, grind The air attrite to fire.

defs: That part of a garment covering the body, as distinguished from the parts covering the limbs.
The bed or box of a vehicle, on or in which the load is placed; as, a wagon body; a cart body.
The shank of a type, or the depth of the shank (by which the size is indicated); as, a nonpareil face on an agate body.
A figure that has length, breadth, and thickness; any solid figure.
Consistency; thickness; substance; strength; as, this color has body; wine of a good body.
notes: ☞ Colors bear a body when they are capable of being ground so fine, and of being mixed so entirely with oil, as to seem only a very thick oil of the same color.

defs: The central, longitudinal framework of a flying machine, to which are attached the planes or aërocurves, passenger accommodations, controlling and propelling apparatus, fuel tanks, etc. Also called fuselage.

quotes: As to the persons who compose the body politic or associate themselves, they take collectively the name of “people”, or “nation”.
Sol gold is, and Luna silver we threpe (=call), Mars yren (=iron), Mercurie quicksilver we clepe, Saturnus lead, and Jupiter is tin, and Venus coper.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bodied (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Bodying.]

defs: To furnish with, or as with, a body; to produce in definite shape; to embody.
quotes: Imagination bodies forth The forms of things unknown.
